name: pool-route
description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router + SessionStart routing-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has вүҘ1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the heavy classifier (make В· cargo В· npm В· gradle В· pytest В· go build В· swift build В· docker build В· nvidia-smi В· train В· hexa kick/drill/loop/cc/run/build В· aprime_cc В· `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan В· вҖҰ) or a root-needing command, the command is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field to run on a pool host over ssh (`ssh <host> 'cd <workdir> && <cmd>'`) вҖ” transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only (0.6.0): the 0.5.7 load-escalation gate вҖ” which promoted any non-trivial cmd to heavy when summed non-claude CPU exceeded 150% вҖ” was removed because on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and shipped local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (0.6.0): `git`/`gh` commands and any `/Users/`В·`/home/` absolute-path literal always run local. Local-bound sign gate (0.6.4): within the absolute-path exemption, HEAVY invocations (hexa CLI any verb В· direct `hexa.real` В· python/python3/py В· `bash`/`sh <script>`) now require a fresh `local` sign-off token вҖ” they were the canonical mac fork-storm trigger (multi-session dispatch-cache races + re-fork on every env-hash miss). USER mints with `! sidecar sign local` in the TUI prompt (5min token, mints freely via `!` bang); the token file `~/.sidecar/signs/local.sign` holds the mint epoch. Without a fresh token the hook emits `permissionDecision: deny` with an instruction trailer. Bare absolute-path reads (ls/cat/grep/find/...) stay free вҖ” the gate narrows to interpreters/compilers only. HOTFIX 0.6.5: (a) `_local_heavy_interp` now matches FIRST-TOKEN basename instead of `_has_word("hexa")` вҖ” 0.6.4 mis-matched `hexa` inside path strings like `/Users/.../hexa-lang/foo.txt`, gating bare reads like `cat <path>`. (b) `hexa run` + `hexa build` added to the heavy classifier вҖ” 0.6.4 missed them, so they ran local on the mac instead of routing to the pool (= mac fork-storm trigger when multi-session). The two together close the false-positive AND false-negative paths. macOS pool-host enable (0.6.6 вҖ” supersedes 0.6.1 Zero-macOS-offload): macOS pool hosts (e.g. `mini` with `os: macos`) are now AUTO-DISPATCH candidates for macOS-only commands (swift build В· xcodebuild В· codesign В· вҖҰ). The 0.6.1 blanket exclusion left compile-heavy work (swift-frontend can drive 85%+ CPU on the workstation) stuck on the local Mac even when an enabled `mini` was idle. New policy: (a) macOS-only commands route to macOS pool hosts; (b) Linux-only commands route to Linux pool hosts (unchanged); (c) general-heavy commands round-robin across Linux pool hosts only вҖ” the workstation Mac stays protected from generic build/test fan-out and is reached only when a macOS-capability marker explicitly demands it. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + `hexa <subverb> --help` dispatchable for kick/drill/loop/cc), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown вҖ” never silently falls back to local. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang`; other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO opt-out by design.
